rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities before assert
Auto-load is filename-based: CLAUDE.md + CLAUDE.local.md at session start
Transcript-by-mtime selection fragile when current session writes target folder
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 69 informs the student that the transcript exists and M5 can find it — no mtime-selection prompt is present in M4; the selection mechanism lives in M5 (learn-from-the-test.md) where it correctly uses SHA-based fallback. No violation here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — 'start a new session', not 'open Claude Code'
Cross-cwd boundaries always new; evidence travels by disk
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Next section (line 69) correctly says 'start a fresh Claude Code session in the same repo'; transcript path claim (~/.claude/projects/) matches rule 6e. No cross-cwd confusion introduced.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label mandatory; maintainer-block scope only
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Pre-reads in body cite without source-type labels (builder voice). Labels appear in maintainer block (line 155) per §1. Correct split.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Freshness 6 months
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Klaassen (Feb 2026) and Entis (Apr 2026) both within 6-month window from 2026-05-14. Uncle Bob supplementary handled via separate file with its own sourcing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Zombie stat guard
Source-verification maintainer block present when practitioner citations exist
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block (line 154–156) carries Source verification section with URL checks flagged and byline attribution confirmed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: ae101-m4-commit-starting-point uses 'm4/<short-task-slug>' which is an angle-bracket template (not square-bracket form banned by rule 1). Claude has task context from session scrollback and infers the slug correctly in practice. Borderline but not a formal violation — Claude proposes from context per rule 1 option (b). Non-blocking observation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All three prompts (commit, send-off, nudge) have lead-in sentences in the module body. Expand confirms canonical shapes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skills by name, never by path
Preamble-before-action structural trigger — 4+ sub-steps needs suppressor or body primer
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: ae101-m4-take-task-end-to-end has ~4 conditional instructions. No in-fence 'No preamble' suppressor and no explicit body preamble-primer after the fence. However: the walk-away mechanic by design makes preamble structurally harmless (student leaves before reading; the 'Go.' terminal imperative signals start). Rule 51 carve-out (deliberate minimal send-off) applies. Non-blocking.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in registry; module files reference by {{prompt:<key>}} marker
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All three prompt references use markers ({{prompt:ae101-m4-commit-starting-point}}, {{prompt:ae101-m4-take-task-end-to-end}}, {{prompt:ae101-m4-nudge-continue}}). Registry files exist at curriculum/prompts/.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No module-name references in prompt prose (bare M-numbers)
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: ae101-m4-commit-starting-point uses 'M4 starting point' in a commit message string (double-quoted literal identifier) — rule 32 exemption (b) applies. ae101-m4-take-task-end-to-end has no module-number references. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Git-output greps with exact-N assertion need multi-result fallback
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No git grep assertions in M4 prompts. The multi-branch disambiguation for reading back the m4/ branch lives in M5 (learn-from-the-test.md line 30) with the body callout correctly placed there.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism check — named tools and skills must exist
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: walk-and-send-off.md exercise exists; test-and-learn.md lecture exists; reading-the-return.md lecture exists; claude-code-for-engineers.md reference exists; clean-code-is-steering.md supplementary exists.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose
Verification machinery needs smoke-test before trusting
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No verification machinery built in this module. N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers as staged progression
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Key Concepts (line 38) correctly claims 'Claude Code loads both [CLAUDE.md + CLAUDE.local.md] at session start' with link to reference. Auto-load claim matches rule 6d. .claude/memory/ is described as non-auto-load (prompt references it explicitly). Correct tier attribution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts in maintainer block
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Artefact contracts table present (lines 135–141) with m4/<task-slug> branch+SHA, .claude/memory/ filled gaps, and ~/.claude/projects/ transcript — all with stable identifiers, producing prompts named, consuming modules named.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch in maintainer block
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch table present (lines 143–167) covering all forcing functions: Connections blocker, Phase 2 over-fill, gap deferral, Huryn-as-lecture, SHA-not-noted, Debrief pre-empt, send-off anxiety.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Leap test (lines 128–132) names three falsifiable Monday-morning outcomes with observable evidence criteria.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Claude Code loads both CLAUDE.md + CLAUDE.local.md at session start
Skills available by name in this session (auto-discovered)
Claude Code stores every session transcript under ~/.claude/projects/ in a folder matching this repo
AE101 is Code-only (no Cowork, no async cloud runner); send-off runs in same Claude Code session
